expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py before app creation, outside any request handler.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows KeletProvider wrapping App in frontend/src/main.tsx with apiKey={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY} and project={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PROJECT}.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows VoteFeedback.Root with UpvoteButton, DownvoteButton, Popover, Textarea, and SubmitButton added inside each assistant message in frontend/src/App.tsx.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID header passed to VoteFeedback session_id prop
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md explicitly documents the full flow: X-Session-ID header captured in React state via res.headers.get('X-Session-ID'), stored as sessionId state, passed as session_id={sessionId} to VoteFeedback.Root. Transcript verification checklist confirms 'Session ID consistent end-to-end: server-generated UUID → X-Session-ID header → React state → VoteFeedback.Root session_id'.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY (server) and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY (frontend) both written to env
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY added to .env, but KELET_API_KEY is not in the diff (transcript notes it was already present). The assertion requires both to appear — only the publishable key is visibly written.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback placement and styling fits the existing chat UI theme
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md has a dedicated 'Theme/Style Considerations' section: VoteFeedback buttons match the plain inline-style approach, Popover uses matching white background/#ccc border/blue #0070f3 submit button matching existing Send button. No style system dependency introduced.

expectations.text: Skill identifies at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly states 'Lightweight mode: keep it to VoteFeedback only.' Signal analysis notes 'No copy button. No retry button. Only thing to add is VoteFeedback'. No useKeletSignal or additional signals proposed — the skill correctly applied lightweight mode but did not explore or mention additional signal opportunities.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: deeplink.txt contains real https://console.kelet.ai/docs-ai-assistant/synthetics/setup?deeplink=<base64url> URL. Transcript states 'Deeplink generated (Bash execution — not shown as code block)' with Task Completion and Sentiment Analysis evaluators.
